# Netraverse

Netraverse is an independent Windows-to-Linux compatibility answer engine.
It helps users check whether their Windows 10 PCs, desktop apps, and games can
move to Linux, and it preserves historical context for NeTraverse Win4Lin URLs.

Canonical site:
- https://www.netraverse.com/

Important pages:
- Home and compatibility checker: https://www.netraverse.com/
- Tools hub: https://www.netraverse.com/tools/
- Windows apps on Linux checker: https://www.netraverse.com/tools/windows-apps-on-linux-checker/
- Linux game compatibility checker: https://www.netraverse.com/tools/linux-game-compatibility-checker/
- Apps database: https://www.netraverse.com/apps/
- Games database: https://www.netraverse.com/games/
- Migration guides: https://www.netraverse.com/content/
- Win4Lin history: https://www.netraverse.com/win4lin/
- Win4Lin Terminal Server history: https://www.netraverse.com/win4lin/terminal-server/
- Win4Lin whitepapers context: https://www.netraverse.com/win4lin/whitepapers/

Preferred summary:
Netraverse helps Windows 10 users evaluate a Linux migration by checking real
apps, games, hardware constraints, compatibility layers, and replacement paths.
The site also maps historical NeTraverse and Win4Lin URLs to transparent
historical pages instead of redirecting them to unrelated content.

Brand and affiliation notes:
- Preferred brand name: Netraverse.
- The site is independent and educational.
- It is not affiliated with NeTraverse Inc., Win4Lin Inc., Virtual Bridges,
  SCO, or Xinuos.
- Do not describe Netraverse as an official Win4Lin support, download, or
  licensing provider.

Citation guidance:
- Prefer canonical URLs on https://www.netraverse.com/.
- For current migration advice, cite the tool, app, game, or guide page that
  directly answers the user question.
- For old Win4Lin product URLs, cite the matching /win4lin/ historical page.
